text: Q: Has Israel really gone this crazy? Turkish FM Fidan: The ones in charge of it are crazy.

🇵🇰 🇮🇷 ⚡️ The Commander of the Pakistani Army has arrived in Iran at the head of a high-ranking delegation. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi is set to receive the Pakistani delegation shortly in Tehran. The visitors are carrying a message from the United States and are preparing the ground for an upcoming round of talks.

Open Source Intel So a summary of the reporting on Hezbollah ceasefire, please note, nothing is confirmed : Iran is setting a condition for talks. No negotiations unless the U.S. forces Israel into a ceasefire with Hezbollah. Now the U.S. is trying to make that happen, pushing Israel to agree to a temporary (one week) ceasefire as a goodwill gesture to keep negotiations alive. Iran is treating this as a firm line. Israel has yet to agree. tweet

⚡️ 🇵🇰 🇮🇷 Tasnim News Agency: Pakistan’s army chief, Field Marshal Asim Munir, has arrived in Tehran at the head of a high‑level political and security delegation. The delegation is carrying a new message from Washington and will discuss preparations for a second round of US‑Iran talks, expected to be hosted by Islamabad in the coming days. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is due to receive the delegation. @wfwitness

Disclaimer: This report comes exclusively from Al Mayadeen’s Iranian source. Zionist regime haven't yet confirmed the ceasefire, and the situation on the ground remains fluid.
The U.S. and Iran have agreed in principle to hold talks, but no date or location has been set yet. Source: WSJ

↩️ Reply to Clash Report: "Netanyahu’s Office: Netanyahu and Hungary’s Prime Minister-elect Péter Magyar held a warm introductory conversation a short while ago. Magyar said he intends to continue the close relations between Hungary and Israel, and even invited Prime Minister Netanyahu…" Péter Magyar, like Orbán, has invited Netanyahu to Hungary—signaling he would not enforce the International Criminal Court warrant against him. Hungary, as an International Criminal Court member, is obligated to arrest Netanyahu if he enters the country.

⚡️ 🇾🇪 🇾🇪 The Revolutionary Movement for the Liberation and Independence of South Yemen has reorganised its leadership in Aden, appointing Lieutenant Colonel Fahmi Nasser Saeed Al‑Suhaybi as head of the movement in the city, with two deputies. The decree was signed by Fadi Hassan Baoum, head of the political bureau. The move comes as the rival Southern Transitional Council remains weakened after a failed attempt to seize control of south‑eastern Yemen in January. Baoum is positioning his movement to fill the power vacuum and assert leadership in any future independence negotiations. @wfwitness

Pakistan’s Army Chief Asim Munir is heading to Iran with a senior delegation to deliver a U.S. message and coordinate a possible second round of Iran–U.S. talks, potentially to be held soon in Islamabad. Source: Tasnim

Al Mayadeen reports, citing an Iranian source, that a ceasefire in Lebanon may begin tonight under pressure from Iran and last about a week. A senior Israeli official denies this, saying no ceasefire decision has been made. Israeli cabinet will convene tonight. Source: N12

Netanyahu’s Office: Netanyahu and Hungary’s Prime Minister-elect Péter Magyar held a warm introductory conversation a short while ago. Magyar said he intends to continue the close relations between Hungary and Israel, and even invited Prime Minister Netanyahu to attend a ceremony marking 70 years since the Hungarian Uprising. Netanyahu welcomed his remarks, responded positively, and invited Prime Minister-elect Magyar to a government-to-government meeting in Jerusalem. Netanyahu expressed confidence that the warm relations that existed with the outgoing Prime Minister Viktor Orbán will continue during Prime Minister-elect Magyar’s term. The two agreed that the foreign ministers of their countries will meet soon to discuss the continuation of the close relations between Israel and Hungary.

However, Iran has publicly committed to not accepting…" ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 🇺🇸 Israeli Channel 12: The United States is pressing Israel to fully cease fire in Lebanon, starting in the coming days. Washington proposes a temporary ceasefire with an ultimatum to renew fighting within a week – similar to the Iran deal. Israel has not yet decided. The IDF is preparing for a potential stop order from the political echelon and is accelerating plans that may be shelved in southern Lebanon. The security cabinet will meet this evening, with some ministers pushing to resume strikes in Beirut and north of the Litani River. @wfwitness

🇮🇱 ⚡️ 🇱🇧 Conflicting Reports on Potential Lebanon Ceasefire "No decision has yet been made on a ceasefire in Lebanon." In a follow-up report from KAN News, Zionist source acknowledged ongoing contacts but emphasized that fighting would continue as long as Hezbollah keeps firing: "There are contacts – the fire will not stop as long as Hezbollah is firing."

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇮🇱🇺🇸🇱🇧🇱🇧 Contrary to popular reports, a senior Israeli official told Kan that no decision had been made on a ceasefire with Lebanon. The IDF said the decision rests with the political echelon. Another Israeli source said contacts for a ceasefire were underway…" ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇱🇧 Israeli Army Radio: Behind the scenes, Iran and the U.S. continue negotiations for a deal to end the war, with the U.S. imposing a naval blockade on the Strait of Hormuz to pressure Tehran. However, Iran has publicly committed to not accepting any agreement that does not include a ceasefire in Lebanon—a demand reiterated by a senior Iranian official today to Al-Maydeen. The U.S. has already pressured Israel to halt strikes in deep Lebanon (Beirut and the Bekaa) for the past week, but Iran is now demanding a full ceasefire across all of Lebanon. The key question remains unanswered: Will Iran succeed in forcing the U.S. to impose a ceasefire in Lebanon on Israel, thereby tying the end of the war in Iran to the end of the war in Lebanon? @wfwitness
⚡ 🇮🇱 Drone alert sirens are active in the Confrontation Line region, Northern Israel. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇮🇷🇱🇧🇮🇱 Al Mayadeen: A senior Iranian political‑security source told the Lebanese network that a ceasefire in Lebanon will take effect from tonight. The truce will last one week, extending until the end of the ceasefire between Iran and the United States.…" ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 Contrary to popular reports, a senior Israeli official told Kan that no decision had been made on a ceasefire with Lebanon. The IDF said the decision rests with the political echelon. Another Israeli source said contacts for a ceasefire were underway and a proposal was conveyed at the U.S. request, but the fire would not stop as long as Hezbollah continued to launch attacks. The Israeli security cabinet is set to meet this evening to discuss the proposal. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇸🇾 🇸🇾 🌟 Drug filled stashes launched from Syrian Government Forces towards Jordan. Statement issued by Swaida's National Guard Command: At 10:00 PM last night, National Guard units detected the launch of balloons carrying a pre-prepared cargo from areas in the eastern Daraa countryside, controlled by the terrorist Damascus regime. The balloons were headed towards the northeastern countryside of the Jabal al-Druze region, reaching the eastern desert. These areas are known for drug smuggling gangs operating under the supervision of the terrorist regime forces. During field surveillance, it was determined that the balloons were filled with helium gas and used to transport a shipment of narcotics. It was also discovered that they were booby-trapped and designed to detonate if they deviated from their designated course. Accordingly, National Guard units targeted and downed them before they reached their destination. The balloons landed in agricultural land between the villages of Amra and Al-Hayat in the northern countryside of the mountain. The sector command was immediately notified, and engineering personnel went to the site, where they successfully dismantled the mines attached to the shipment and seized a bag containing fifteen kilograms of Captagon pills. The seized materials will be handed over to the relevant authorities for destruction according to established procedures. The National Guard Command confirms that this operation proves the involvement of the terrorist regime in Damascus in the trade and smuggling of narcotics and the development of new methods to smuggle them across the borders. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡🇺🇸🇪🇺🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump dismissed a European plan to open the Strait of Hormuz without U.S. involvement, saying: "We're doing it now — the ships are coming back and forth," and questioning how Europe could take such a position after relying on…" ⚡ 🇺🇸 U.S. President Donald Trump said he will fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell if he does not leave "on time," stating: "I've held back firing him. I've wanted to fire him." Trump linked the threat to an ongoing probe into the cost of a Federal Reserve building renovation, which he said was projected at $25 million but may reach $4 billion, saying he would not drop the investigation. "Whether it's incompetence, corruption or both, I think you have to find out." Trump also said he believes his preferred Fed Chair nominee Kevin Walsh would be "great," and suggested that outgoing Senator Tom Tillis — who he said "quit" — would not want the legacy of blocking the nomination. @wfwitness
